question: Ilha  is a civil parish in the municipality of Santana in a Portoguese archipelago that had how many inhabitants in 2011 ?

hop1_docs: Ilha (Santana) | Ilha (Portuguese for "island") is a civil parish in the municipality of Santana in the Portuguese archipelago of Madeira.  The population in 2011 was 255, in an area of 14.19 km².

Guia, Ilha e Mata Mourisca | Guia, Ilha e Mata Mourisca is a civil parish in the municipality of Pombal, Portugal.  It was formed in 2013 by the merger of the former parishes Guia, Ilha and Mata Mourisca.  The population in 2011 was 6,489, in an area of 80.37 km².

Faial (Santana) | Faial is a civil parish in the Madeiran municipality of Santana located along the northern coast of the island of Madeira.  The population in 2011 was 1,567, in an area of 24.03 km².
São Jorge (Santana) | São Jorge (Portuguese: "Saint George" ) is a civil parish in the municipality of Santana, in the island of Madeira.  The population in 2011 was 1,473, in an area of 19.34 km².

Nossa Senhora do Rosário | Nossa Senhora do Rosário is a civil parish and most populated area in the municipality of Lagoa, on the island of São Miguel, in the Portuguese archipelago of the Azores.  With a population of 5396 inhabitants in 2011, the parish covers an area of approximately 6.52 km2 that extends from the southern coast of the island.

Arco de São Jorge | Arco de São Jorge is a civil parish in the municipality of Santana, the Portuguese island of Madeira.  The population in 2011 was 413, in an area of 3.38 km²; it is the smallest parish of the municipality by population and area.

Ilha de Santana Lighthouse | Ilha de Santana Lighthouse (Portuguese: "Farol da Ilha de Santana" ) is an active lighthouse on the namesake island located at the east entrance of the Baía de São Marcos, Maranhão, Brazil
hop2_docs: Ilha (Santana) | Ilha (Portuguese for "island") is a civil parish in the municipality of Santana in the Portuguese archipelago of Madeira.  The population in 2011 was 255, in an area of 14.19 km².

Guia, Ilha e Mata Mourisca | Guia, Ilha e Mata Mourisca is a civil parish in the municipality of Pombal, Portugal.  It was formed in 2013 by the merger of the former parishes Guia, Ilha and Mata Mourisca.  The population in 2011 was 6,489, in an area of 80.37 km².

Ilha de Santana Lighthouse | Ilha de Santana Lighthouse (Portuguese: "Farol da Ilha de Santana" ) is an active lighthouse on the namesake island located at the east entrance of the Baía de São Marcos, Maranhão, Brazil
Ilha | Ilha (Portuguese for "island") may refer to the following places in Portugal or Mozambique:

Guia (Pombal) | Guia is a former civil parish in the municipality of Pombal, Portugal.  In 2013, the parish merged into the new parish Guia, Ilha e Mata Mourisca.  It has a population of 2,726 (2001) and an area of 37.91 km².

Faial (Santana) | Faial is a civil parish in the Madeiran municipality of Santana located along the northern coast of the island of Madeira.  The population in 2011 was 1,567, in an area of 24.03 km².
São Jorge (Santana) | São Jorge (Portuguese: "Saint George" ) is a civil parish in the municipality of Santana, in the island of Madeira.  The population in 2011 was 1,473, in an area of 19.34 km².
